summary: I compared the built diff against the 18 business requirements from Define for the "Live pipeline status summary" feature. The core is `Pipelines::StatusSummary`, a pure, total derivation PORO whose first-match ladder maps whole-pipeline state to one plain-language, never-blank sentence (R3–R13), rendered by a single shared partial in the detail-page header above the step cards (R1) and compactly in each list row (R2) so the two surfaces can never disagree (R18); status is carried by words with the tone dot marked `aria-hidden` (R17). Live delivery uses after-commit `Pipelines::BroadcastStatus` reload-and-rerender broadcasts (order-independent, R16) wired into step claim/complete/progress and manager-tick, and both controllers preload `with_board` so pages render true state on load without a socket (R15). Critically, the one gap from the previous iteration — the human gate-approval path (`Phases::Approve` → `Advance`) not pushing an update when a gate *stops* waiting (R14) — has been fixed: `Phases::Approve` now calls `Pipelines::BroadcastStatus.call(pipeline)` after advancing. With every requirement satisfied and evidenced by code plus integration/unit/broadcast tests, I wrote a **pass** verdict with empty findings.
